report_name: Atlassian and Splunk Push Critical Patches for RCE and Dependency Flaws

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-20266
vulnerabilities.description: A critical OS command injection vulnerability in the btool configuration helper of the Splunk AI Toolkit for Splunk Enterprise. The flaw allows a remote, authenticated administrator to execute arbitrary OS commands on the host OS due to unsanitized user-supplied parameters.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-20265
vulnerabilities.description: A medium-severity information disclosure vulnerability in the Splunk AI Toolkit for Splunk Enterprise due to an insecure default domain allowlist.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-42043
vulnerabilities.description: A critical vulnerability in the Axios third-party library, affecting various Atlassian products as part of a supply chain vulnerability patch release.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-41293
vulnerabilities.description: A critical vulnerability in the Apache Tomcat third-party component, affecting various Atlassian products as part of a supply chain vulnerability patch release.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-42584
vulnerabilities.description: A critical vulnerability in the Netty third-party library, affecting various Atlassian products as part of a supply chain vulnerability patch release.

indicators.name: Suspicious Windows Shell Spawned by Splunk Daemon
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Windows Shell Spawned by Splunk Daemon
id: 2b1c3d4e-5f6a-4b8c-9d0e-1f2a3b4c5d6e
status: experimental
description: Detects the Splunk daemon (splunkd.exe) spawning a Windows shell process, which could indicate exploitation of a command injection vulnerability like CVE-2026-20266.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/atlassian-splunk-patch-critical-vulnerabilities/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-18
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.execution
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts or Splunk apps that execute shell commands.
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\splunkd.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the Splunk daemon (splunkd.exe) on Windows spawning a shell process, which could indicate exploitation of a command injection vulnerability like CVE-2026-20266.

indicators.name: Suspicious Linux Shell Spawned by Splunk Daemon
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Linux Shell Spawned by Splunk Daemon
id: 8e9f0a1b-2c3d-4e5f-6a7b-8c9d0e1f2a3b
status: experimental
description: Detects the Splunk daemon (splunkd) spawning a Linux shell process, which could indicate exploitation of a command injection vulnerability like CVE-2026-20266.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/atlassian-splunk-patch-critical-vulnerabilities/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-18
tags:
  - attack.t1059.004
  - attack.execution
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts or Splunk apps that execute shell commands.
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '/splunkd'
    Image|endswith:
      - '/bash'
      - '/sh'
      - '/zsh'
      - '/ksh'
      - '/csh'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the Splunk daemon (splunkd) on Linux spawning a shell process, which could indicate exploitation of a command injection vulnerability like CVE-2026-20266.

indicators.name: Suspicious Shell Spawned by Atlassian Java Process
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Shell Spawned by Atlassian Java Process
id: 4c5d6e7f-8a9b-4c0d-1e2f-3a4b5c6d7e8f
status: experimental
description: Detects a Java process, likely related to an Atlassian product (Jira, Confluence), spawning a shell. This could indicate RCE from a compromised third-party dependency.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/atlassian-splunk-patch-critical-vulnerabilities/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-18
tags:
  - attack.t1059
  - attack.execution
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate application functionality in Jira or Confluence that executes shell commands (e.g., scripts, plugins). High potential for FPs, requires tuning.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\java.exe'
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
      - 'atlassian.jira'
      - 'atlassian.confluence'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a Java process associated with an Atlassian product spawning a shell, which could indicate RCE due to a dependency flaw.
